nodes.dna_json: {"title":"Define Task Activation Responsibility","description":"PDSA AGENT: Analyze and document who activates tasks (pending → active). dna.rule: PDSA plans and tests. Dev implements. PDSA must NEVER complete task nodes directly.

nodes.dna_json: {"title":"Fix Visualization Structure - Stations by Type","description":"Current visualization is WRONG. Packages flow through status stations (pending/ready/active/done). requirements: Stations are: requirement, design, task, test (and other node types)

nodes.dna_json: {"title":"Document PDSA and QA as Separate Roles","description":"CORRECTION: PDSA and QA are TWO separate roles, not one. Current situation: 1 agent handles both jobs. nodes.dna_json: {"title":"Agent-Station Visualization Redesign (Object-Oriented)","description":"Revise the agent-station design based on Thomas feedback. The current design is missing the object-oriented station approach.","thomas_feedback":{"verbatim":"the website does not know what agent or human is in which station. its a sandbox. it gets loaded by the operator when we start working on a project. that is: 0 tasks at start. orchastrator loads project and station receives object that contains all tasks information, unpacks it and puts information into the column. then when pdsa agent startmonitoring there must be a logon functionality through the mcp server that he is now taking care of a certain role. then the station knows that and assigns the agent on the page for visualisation and so on - think on that line. object orientation. station in a warehouse. objects that are loading self into the station that then can visualze those properties"},"requirements":["Station is a sandbox - starts with 0 tasks","Orchestrator loads project -> station receives self-describing objects","Objects unpack themselves and display properties in the column","Agent logon functionality via MCP - agent takes responsibility for a role","Station visualizes which agent is logged into which role","Object-oriented approach - stations receive objects, not just data"],"acceptance_criteria":["Design documents station as sandbox concept","Design shows object loading flow (orchestrator -> station -> unpack)","Design includes MCP schema for agent logon","Design shows how station visualizes agent assignments","Design is approved by Thomas before implementation"],"previous_design":"viz-agent-stations-design","pdsa_ref":"docs/pdsa/2026-02-03-viz-agent-stations-design.pdsa.md"}

dna.title: Agent-Station Visualization Redesign (Object-Oriented)
dna.description: Revise the agent-station design based on Thomas feedback. The current design is missing the object-oriented station approach.

thomas_feedback.verbatim: the website does not know what agent or human is in which station. its a sandbox. it gets loaded by the operator when we start working on a project. that is: 0 tasks at start. orchastrator loads project and station receives object that contains all tasks information, unpacks it and puts information into the column. then when pdsa agent startmonitoring there must be a logon functionality through the mcp server that he is now taking care of a certain role. then the station knows that and assigns the agent on the page for visualisation and so on - think on that line. object orientation. station in a warehouse. objects that are loading self into the station that then can visualze those properties

requirements: Station is a sandbox - starts with 0 tasks
Orchestrator loads project -> station receives self-describing objects
Objects unpack themselves and display properties in the column
Agent logon functionality via MCP - agent takes responsibility for a role
Station visualizes which agent is logged into which role
Object-oriented approach - stations receive objects, not just data
acceptance_criteria: Design documents station as sandbox concept
Design shows object loading flow (orchestrator -> station -> unpack)
Design includes MCP schema for agent logon
Design shows how station visualizes agent assignments
Design is approved by Thomas before implementation

nodes.dna_json: {"title":"Implement Station-Based Visualization","description":"Implement the object-oriented station visualization per PDSA design.","pdsa_ref":"docs/pdsa/2026-02-03-viz-agent-stations-redesign.pdsa.md","implementation_sequence":["1. Create stations table in SQLite schema","2. Insert 5 default stations (pdsa, dev, qa, orchestrator, human)","3. Update viz/export-data.js to include stations in data.json","4. Refactor viz/index.html to show warehouse layout (stations, queue, completed)","5. Add agent logon display (show agent_id on station when logged on)","6. Add current object display (show loaded object in station)"],"acceptance_criteria":["stations table exists with 5 default stations","data.json includes stations array","Visualization shows stations as sandboxes","Queue area shows pending objects","Completed area shows finished objects","Logged-on agents shown on their stations"],"files_to_modify":["data/schema.sql or direct SQL","viz/export-data.js","viz/index.html"]}

dna.title: Implement Station-Based Visualization
dna.description: Implement the object-oriented station visualization per PDSA design.
implementation_sequence: 1. Create stations table in SQLite schema
2. Insert 5 default stations (pdsa, dev, qa, orchestrator, human)
3. Update viz/export-data.js to include stations in data.json
4. Refactor viz/index.html to show warehouse layout (stations, queue, completed)
5. Add agent logon display (show agent_id on station when logged on)
6. Add current object display (show loaded object in station)
acceptance_criteria: stations table exists with 5 default stations
data.json includes stations array
Visualization shows stations as sandboxes
Queue area shows pending objects
Completed area shows finished objects
Logged-on agents shown on their stations

nodes.dna_json:
{"title":"Node Highlight and Navigation Visual Feedback","description":"Requirements for visualization interactivity:

1. When clicking on a node, it needs to HIGHLIGHT (visual feedback)
2. When clicking a child link or parent link, it should SWITCH the highlight to the clicked target (follow the navigation)
3. Nodes need to be visualized by their current station - when PDSA agent is working on a requirement, it shows in PDSA station, NOT in Queue (pending)

Thomas's words:
> "when i am clicking on a node then it needs to highlight. when i am then doing child link or parent link then it shall also switch corresponding to that i have clicked as the next target"
> "when the pdsa agent is working on a requirement it is visualized then not in Queue (pending) but in the station PDSA"","priority":"high"}

dna.title: Node Highlight and Navigation Visual Feedback
dna.description:
Requirements for visualization interactivity:

1. When clicking on a node, it needs to HIGHLIGHT (visual feedback)
2. When clicking a child link or parent link, it should SWITCH the highlight to the clicked target (follow the navigation)
3. Nodes need to be visualized by their current station - when PDSA agent is working on a requirement, it shows in PDSA station, NOT in Queue (pending)

Thomas's words:
> "when i am clicking on a node then it needs to highlight. when i am then doing child link or parent link then it shall also switch corresponding to that i have clicked as the next target"
> "when the pdsa agent is working on a requirement it is visualized then not in Queue (pending) but in the station PDSA"

nodes.dna_json: {"title":"Design: Node Highlight and Station-Based Visualization","description":"PDSA design for node highlight, navigation feedback, and station-based node placement.","requirement_ref":"2e552a57-c185-4eb5-aeca-d4f66b6d7464","thomas_feedback":{"highlight":"when i am clicking on a node then it needs to highlight. when i am then doing child link or parent link then it shall also switch corresponding to that i have clicked as the next target","station_placement":"when the pdsa agent is working on a requirement it is visualized then not in Queue (pending) but in the station PDSA"},"design_sections":["Node highlight visual style","Highlight switching on parent/child navigation","Station-based node placement (active work shows in station, not queue)"],"acceptance_criteria":["Clicking node highlights it visually","Clicking parent/child link switches highlight to target","Nodes with active work show in the working station, not Queue","Design approved by Thomas before implementation"],"pdsa_ref":{"git":"https://github.com/PichlerThomas/xpollination-mcp-server/blob/main/docs/pdsa/2026-02-03-viz-node-highlight-nav-design.pdsa.md","workspace":"/home/developer/workspaces/github/PichlerThomas/xpollination-mcp-server/docs/pdsa/2026-02-03-viz-node-highlight-nav-design.pdsa.md"},"thomas_answers":{"highlight_style":"PDSA choice: border+glow","auto_scroll":"Yes - auto-scroll when navigating to off-screen node","station_empty_state":"Leave empty as is"}}

thomas_feedback.station_placement: when the pdsa agent is working on a requirement it is visualized then not in Queue (pending) but in the station PDSA

nodes.dna_json:
{"title":"Create Onboarding Script for New Projects","description":"Create scripts/onboard-project.sh in xpollination-mcp-server

Thomas's answers:
> "1. leave empty. finish xpollination-mcp-server and then we continue with the first node of the homepage"
> "2. yes good idea to preserve this knowledge. add to xpollination-mcp-server (pdsa to be precise and will remember context!)"
> "3. can point to mcp server. is a good approach as changes on this project ripple through all projects using this method"

Requirements:
1. Script takes project path as argument
2. Creates data/xpollination.db using schema from mcp-server
3. Initializes default stations
4. Verifies setup

Schema approach: Reference mcp-server schema (changes ripple to all projects)","priority":"medium"}

dna.title: Create Onboarding Script for New Projects
dna.description:
Create scripts/onboard-project.sh in xpollination-mcp-server

Thomas's answers:
> "1. leave empty. finish xpollination-mcp-server and then we continue with the first node of the homepage"
> "2. yes good idea to preserve this knowledge. add to xpollination-mcp-server (pdsa to be precise and will remember context!)"
> "3. can point to mcp server. is a good approach as changes on this project ripple through all projects using this method"

Requirements:
1. Script takes project path as argument
2. Creates data/xpollination.db using schema from mcp-server
3. Initializes default stations
4. Verifies setup

Schema approach: Reference mcp-server schema (changes ripple to all projects)
